# SignalScout

A single-page web application that helps rural/off-grid users determine which nearby cell tower to aim a directional antenna at — by visualizing terrain profiles, line-of-sight (LOS), Fresnel-zone clearance, and knife-edge diffraction loss between a target location and user-supplied cell tower locations.

## How It Works

1. Enter your target location (latitude, longitude)
2. Add nearby cell tower coordinates (sourced from cellmapper.net)
3. Click "Analyze paths" to see terrain profiles, clearance analysis, and ranked tower recommendations

## Details

- **Map**: OpenStreetMap via Leaflet
- **Elevation data**: Open-Meteo Elevation API (Copernicus GLO-90 DEM, 90 m resolution)
- **Analysis**: Haversine distance, line-of-sight, 60% first-Fresnel-zone clearance, single knife-edge diffraction loss (ITU-R P.526), free-space path loss
- **Frequency bands**: 700 / 850 / 1800 / 2100 / 2600 / 3500 MHz

## Links

- [SignalScout](https://signalscout.yob-yob.com/) — main application
- [Source code](https://github.com/anomalyco/SignalScout) — GitHub repository
